title: Apple pays $250M for Siri features that didn't ship
desc: Daily AI news satire. Apple settles for $250M over a Siri 2.0 it announced at WWDC 2024 but never shipped. The new one ships at WWDC 2026 — powered by Google's Gemini.

beats.name: platform-cold-open
beats.narration: Apple has agreed to pay two hundred and fifty million dollars.
beats.cinematography: Slow zoom-in on the lone commuter (1.0x -> 1.04x)

beats.name: crowd-of-ticket-holders
beats.narration: The class-action suit was brought by iPhone owners who paid for AI features that were never delivered.
beats.cinematography: Static — let the crowd reveal carry it

beats.name: conductor-bows
beats.narration: The features were unveiled at WWDC 2024, with a planned release later that year.
beats.cinematography: Static — the bow lands on its own

beats.name: time-skip-2025
beats.narration: Reports indicate the personalized Siri was originally targeted for the iOS 18 cycle.
beats.cinematography: Subtle drift; same crowd visibly aged

beats.name: fresh-commuter-joins
beats.narration: During the two-year wait, Apple released the iPhone 16 and the iPhone 17 — each marketed on the strength of the unshipped feature.
beats.cinematography: Slow zoom-out as the new commuter joins (1.04x -> 1.0x)

beats.name: lawyer-arrives
beats.narration: A settlement was reached this week.
beats.cinematography: Subtle push-in on the lawyer's papers

beats.name: briefcase-reveals-cash
beats.narration: Per the filing Apple admits no wrongdoing.
beats.cinematography: **Punchline beat — extra dwell.** Static. Let the briefcase open and the cash sit on screen.

beats.name: willens-calculator
beats.narration: Divided across affected iPhone 15 Pro and 16 owners, the payout works out to approximately twenty-five dollars each.
beats.cinematography: Static — Willens shrugging at the calculator

beats.name: pichai-gemini-orb
beats.narration: The revamped Siri is scheduled to debut at WWDC on June eighth. Per Bloomberg, it will be powered by Google's Gemini.
beats.cinematography: Slow drift on Pichai handing the orb forward

beats.name: board-flips-fresh-commuters
beats.cinematography: Static — board flips, fresh commuters fill in

beats.name: ronin-commuter-closing
beats.cinematography: Slow zoom-out on the original commuter walking into shadow (1.0x -> 1.06x over full 4s)

sources.attribution: Lawsuit over delayed Siri features reaches $250M settlement (May 5, 2026)
Apple to pay $250M to settle lawsuit over delayed AI-powered Siri (May 6, 2026)
Apple may have just made one of the most important new Siri announcements (May 6, 2026)
Apple Plans to Open Up Siri to Rival AI Assistants Beyond ChatGPT in iOS 27 (March 26, 2026)
Apple confirms Siri 2.0 is still coming in 2026

transcript: Apple has agreed to pay two hundred and fifty million dollars. The class-action suit was brought by iPhone owners who paid for AI features that were never delivered. The features were unveiled at WWDC 2024, with a planned release later that year. Reports indicate the personalized Siri was originally targeted for the iOS 18 cycle. During the two-year wait, Apple released the iPhone 16 and the iPhone 17 — each marketed on the strength of the unshipped feature. Two years passed. A settlement was reached this week. Per the filing Apple admits no wrongdoing. Divided across affected iPhone 15 Pro and 16 owners, the payout works out to approximately twenty-five dollars each. The revamped Siri is scheduled to debut at WWDC on June eighth. Per Bloomberg, it will be powered by Google's Gemini. Reservations are open. Soon.

writing.author: Claude
